steps.title: Identify what the claim requires
steps.content:
The passage says Cather portrays Alexandra as having a deep emotional connection to her natural surroundings. We need a quotation that shows inner feeling or attachment to nature — not practical skill, not action, not interaction with other people.
  • Choice A — "She had never known before how much the country meant to her. The chirping of the insects down in the long grass had been like the sweetest music. She had felt as if her heart were hiding down there, somewhere, with the quail and the plover and all the little wild things that crooned or buzzed in the sun. Under the long shaggy ridges, she felt the future stirring.": Shows Alexandra’s interior emotional bond — the land “meant” something to her, her “heart were hiding” in the grass, she “felt the future stirring.” ✓
  • Choice B — "Alexandra talked to the men about their crops and to the women about their poultry. She spent a whole day with one young farmer who had been away at school, and who was experimenting with a new kind of clover hay. She learned a great deal.": Describes her interacting with neighbors to learn about farming — practical, social, no emotional connection to nature. ✗
  • Choice C — "Alexandra drove off alone. The rattle of her wagon was lost in the howling of the wind, but her lantern, held firmly between her feet, made a moving point of light along the highway, going deeper and deeper into the dark country.": Depicts her driving at night; describes physical action with no mention of her emotional state. ✗
  • Choice D — "It was Alexandra who read the papers and followed the markets, and who learned by the mistakes of their neighbors. It was Alexandra who could always tell about what it had cost to fatten each steer, and who could guess the weight of a hog before it went on the scales closer than John Bergson [her father] himself.": Shows her business acumen and market knowledge — again practical, not emotional connection to nature. ✗

steps.title: Select the matching choice
steps.content: Choice A — "She had never known before how much the country meant to her. The chirping of the insects down in the long grass had been like the sweetest music. She had felt as if her heart were hiding down there, somewhere, with the quail and the plover and all the little wild things that crooned or buzzed in the sun. Under the long shaggy ridges, she felt the future stirring." gives language of interior feeling: the country “meant” something to her, her heart was “hiding” in the landscape, and she “felt” the future stirring under the ridges — all direct evidence of emotional connection to her surroundings. The emotional-connection evidence is correct.
